title: Teachers Get Their Special Day!
story: Every October 5, students around the world celebrate World Teachers' Day by showing extra appreciation for their educators. Kids write thank-you notes, draw pictures, and bring small gifts like apples or flowers to school. Many schools organize special assemblies where students perform songs or skits for their teachers. Some countries even give teachers the day off as a holiday!
activity_question: What do students commonly bring to teachers on World Teachers' Day?
follow_up_question: What's the most creative way you could thank a teacher who made a difference in your life?

title: Nice Day for Being Nice!
story: Every October 5, people across the country celebrate National Do Something Nice Day by spreading kindness wherever they go! Some folks surprise their neighbors with homemade cookies or help carry groceries. Kids write thank-you notes to their teachers, hold doors open for strangers, or give compliments to friends. Social media fills up with photos of good deeds and acts of kindness. The holiday reminds everyone that being nice doesn't cost anything, but it can make someone's whole day brighter. Whether it's sharing lunch, helping with homework, or simply smiling at someone who looks sad, every small act counts on this special day!
activity_question: What are some ways people celebrate National Do Something Nice Day?
activity_choices: Writing thank-you notes and helping neighbors
Eating ice cream and watching movies
activity_answer: Writing thank-you notes and helping neighbors
follow_up_question: What's the nicest thing someone has ever done for you?

title: Apple Betty Makes Everything Better!
story: Every October 5, dessert lovers celebrate National Apple Betty Day by baking this cozy American treat! Apple Betty is like apple pie's simpler cousin - layers of sweet apples mixed with buttery breadcrumbs and warm spices like cinnamon. People share their family recipes online, host baking parties, and fill their kitchens with amazing smells. Some bakeries offer special Apple Betty flavors, while families pass down recipes that are decades old.
activity_question: What makes Apple Betty different from apple pie?
activity_choices: It uses breadcrumbs instead of pie crust
It's made with green apples only
It's served frozen like ice cream
activity_answer: It uses breadcrumbs instead of pie crust
follow_up_question: What other simple desserts could have their own special day?

title: Get Down and Get Funky Day!
story: Every October 5, people across the country celebrate National Get Funky Day by letting their wild side shine! This awesome holiday encourages everyone to break free from boring routines and express themselves in totally unique ways. People wear crazy outfits with bright colors, funky patterns, and mismatched socks. They dance to groovy music, try new hairstyles, and share their most creative selfies on social media. Some folks learn funky dance moves, while others experiment with bold makeup or temporary tattoos. The whole point is to be yourself and have fun doing it! Whether you're rocking platform shoes or just doing a silly dance in your room, this day is all about embracing your inner funkiness.
activity_question: What do people wear to celebrate National Get Funky Day?
follow_up_question: What's the funkiest outfit you could put together from your closet?

title: Celebrating Our Digital Heroes Day
story: Every October 5, we celebrate National Techies Day to honor the amazing people who keep our digital world running! Tech experts, computer programmers, IT specialists, and gadget gurus get special recognition for fixing our phones, creating cool apps, and solving tricky computer problems. Many companies throw pizza parties for their tech teams, while kids post thank-you messages to their favorite tech helpers on social media. Some schools even invite local techies to share how they got started with coding and computers, inspiring the next generation of digital problem-solvers!
activity_question: What do many companies do to celebrate their tech teams on National Techies Day?
follow_up_question: What kind of tech problem would you want to learn how to solve?
